parts.text:
```json
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "question": "Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for logical addressing (IP addresses)?",
    "options": [
      "Data Link Layer",
      "Network Layer",
      "Transport Layer",
      "Application Layer"
    ],
    "answer": "Network Layer"
  },
  {
    "id": 2,
    "question": "What is the primary function of a router?",
    "options": [
      "To connect devices within a single local area network (LAN)",
      "To filter network traffic based on MAC addresses",
      "To forward data packets between different computer networks",
      "To convert digital signals to analog signals"
    ],
    "answer": "To forward data packets between different computer networks"
  },
  {
    "id": 3,
    "question": "Which protocol is connection-oriented and provides reliable data transmission?",
    "options": [
      "UDP (User Datagram Protocol)",
      "IP (Internet Protocol)",
      "TCP (Transmission Control Protocol)",
      "ICMP (Internet Control Message Protocol)"
    ],
    "answer": "TCP (Transmission Control Protocol)"
  },
  {
    "id": 4,
    "question": "What is the default port number for HTTP?",
    "options": [
      "21",
      "23",
      "80",
      "443"
    ],
    "answer": "80"
  },
  {
    "id": 5,
    "question": "A MAC address operates at which layer of the OSI model?",
    "options": [
      "Physical Layer",
      "Data Link Layer",
      "Network Layer",
      "Transport Layer"
    ],
    "answer": "Data Link Layer"
  },
  {
    "id": 6,
    "question": "Which of the following is a private IPv4 address range according to RFC 1918?",
    "options": [
      "10.0.0.0 - 10.255.255.255",
      "172.0.0.0 - 172.15.255.255",
      "192.167.0.0 - 192.167.255.255",
      "203.0.113.0 - 203.0.113.255"
    ],
    "answer": "10.0.0.0 - 10.255.255.255"
  },
  {
    "id": 7,
    "question": "What does DHCP stand for?",
    "options": [
      "Dynamic Host Control Protocol",
      "Dynamic Hypertext Configuration Protocol",
      "Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol",
      "Data Host Communication Protocol"
    ],
    "answer": "Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol"
  },
  {
    "id": 8,
    "question": "Which network topology connects all devices to a central hub or switch?",
    "options": [
      "Bus Topology",
      "Ring Topology",
      "Star Topology",
      "Mesh Topology"
    ],
    "answer": "Star Topology"
  },
  {
    "id": 9,
    "question": "What is the purpose of DNS?",
    "options": [
      "To assign IP addresses dynamically",
      "To translate domain names to IP addresses",
      "To secure web traffic",
      "To route packets between networks"
    ],
    "answer": "To translate domain names to IP addresses"
  },
  {
    "id": 10,
    "question": "Which command is used to test the reachability of a host on an Internet Protocol (IP) network?",
    "options": [
      "ipconfig",
      "tracert",
      "ping",
      "netstat"
    ],
    "answer": "ping"
  },
  {
    "id": 11,
    "question": "How many layers are there in the standard TCP/IP model?",
    "options": [
      "3",
      "4",
      "5",
      "7"
    ],
    "answer": "4"
  },
  {
    "id": 12,
    "question": "Which device typically works at Layer 2 (Data Link Layer) of the OSI model and uses MAC addresses to forward frames?",
    "options": [
      "Router",
      "Hub",
      "Switch",
      "Repeater"
    ],
    "answer": "Switch"
  },
  {
    "id": 13,
    "question": "What does LAN stand for?",
    "options": [
      "Large Area Network",
      "Local Area Network",
      "Long Access Network",
      "Logical Area Node"
    ],
    "answer": "Local Area Network"
  },
  {

    "question": "Which protocol is commonly used for securely transferring files over an SSH connection?",

      "TFTP",
      "SFTP",

  {

    "question": "What is the primary function of a firewall?",

      "To connect different network segments",
      "To prevent unauthorized access to a network",
      "To assign IP addresses to devices"
    ],
    "answer": "To prevent unauthorized access to a network"
  },
  {
    "id": 16,
    "question": "What is the common term for a unique identifier assigned to network interfaces for communications within a network segment?",
    "options": [
      "IP Address",
      "Subnet Mask",
      "MAC Address",
      "Gateway Address"

    "answer": "MAC Address"

  {

    "question": "Which port number is typically used by HTTPS?",

  },
  {
    "id": 19,
    "question": "Which of the following is an example of a broadcast address for a Class C network (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24)?",

    "question": "What does VPN stand for?",
```
